findings.finding: install_guard.py timing-window shape changes pre/post fix; plan dismisses as pre-existing without verifying the wider window doesn't change observable behavior
findings.evidence_quote: The known transient "stale-active-session" warning during a takeover window in install_guard.py is acknowledged as a pre-existing limitation, not introduced by this fix

findings.finding: ConcurrentRunnerError reuses existing_pid=-1 sentinel that already means 'third party beat us on recreate path'; new lock-timeout collapses two distinct failures onto one wire signal
findings.evidence_quote: On budget exhaustion, raise ConcurrentRunnerError(existing_session_id="<unknown>", existing_pid=-1) with the explicit timeout message

findings.finding: JSON error-code contract gap: cancel --format json emits versioned {error: <code>} payloads but the lock-timeout case has no specified error code value
findings.evidence_quote: when non-force cancel exhausts the 5 s budget, ConcurrentRunnerError carries the same explicit timeout message from Task 2

findings.finding: install_guard.py:74-104 has separate argparse for --force; Task 4 doesn't enumerate it as touched or mandate drift test
findings.evidence_quote: install_guard.py already references this invocation form

findings.finding: --force help= string is unspecified; only discoverable semantics live in plan prose
findings.evidence_quote: Add cancel.add_argument("--force", action="store_true", default=False, ...) to the overnight cancel parser

findings.finding: --force session-id resolution semantics not specified; JSON-mode error rendering for resolution-failure path under --force unspecified
findings.evidence_quote: --force accepts a <100 ms "no active session" race window during a concurrent takeover

findings.finding: Task 9 ships discipline rules as doc prose; project convention enforces invariants by pre-commit gates (bin/cortex-check-parity per requirements/project.md:27); no static gate task included
findings.evidence_quote: states the discipline rules (never written, never unlinked by production code, never durable_fsync'd, must NOT be matched by *.lock glob auto-cleanup)

findings.finding: V1 enumerates Linux-access options for Task 7 but no parallel veto entry for Task 8's dual-sha-deploy executability problem; Task 8 sits at chain end so escalation blocks ticket close-out
findings.evidence_quote: V1 (Linux R9 access): Task 7's R9 one-shot 1000-iteration validation requires the implementer to run the stress test on both macOS and Linux
